title: Witheringia glandulosa
taxonomy: Witheringia glandulosa is classified within the family Solanaceae, commonly known as the nightshade family. This family includes a wide variety of flowering plants, many of which are found in tropical and subtropical regions. The genus Witheringia is a small group of flowering plants, with Witheringia glandulosa being one of the species within this genus. Witheringia glandulosa is native to South America, where it is found in the Andes mountain range. It is a shrub or small tree that produces small, white flowers and red berries.

intro: Witheringia glandulosa, also known as Yerba de la Corte, is a plant species native to South America, commonly used for its medicinal properties. The health benefits of Witheringia glandulosa include its potential to reduce inflammation, alleviate pain, and exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant activities, making it a valuable resource for traditional medicine.
